# Css.gg

- **Source**: https://github.com/astrit/css.gg
- **Site**: https://css.gg
- **Author**: Astrit
- **License**: **Custom, NOT MIT from v2.1.2+** (see `LICENSE.md`)
- **Version fetched**: depth-1 clone, 2026-04-21
- **Icon count**: 704 SVGs
- **Style**: 8x8 pixel-grid minimal glyphs, unusually geometric
- **Raw SVG path**: `icons/svg/`

## Contents

- `icons/svg/` — 704 SVG files
- `icons/icons.js` — JS export manifest
- `icons/icons.json` — icon metadata json
- `LICENSE.md` — custom license
- `README.md` — upstream readme

## License caveat (READ BEFORE USE)

**Post-2.1.2 license is not MIT.** Operator-facing summary:

- Attribution to "Astrit" + CSS.GG/GLYF.APP brand names required
- Personal, non-commercial use allowed
- Commercial use requires explicit permission
- No derivative works / no functional copy

Consolidate use case is internal/non-commercial/fair-use per operator's framing, which fits the allowed-uses clause. If Clawmachine ever becomes a commercial redistribution, this set either needs explicit permission or needs to be dropped.

Pre-2.1.2 versions are MIT but not what was cloned here.

## Consolidate notes

- Style is the reason to keep this: each icon is built with CSS primitives, giving them a rigid 8px-grid look that rhymes with Pixelarticons and Mac OS 9 UI. Visually orthogonal to Feather/Lucide.
- Good candidate for retro-cyberpunk accent icons, not primary UI.
